crime.value: Murder by stabbing - Matt killed his grandfather Johnsen with a knife on a boat in the middle of Dead Mans Lake
crime.quote: he pulled out his knife and started stabbing Johnsen over and over again in the chest

victim.value: Johnsen (grandfather) who was apparently attempting to harm or kill Matt during a nighttime boat trip
victim.quote: As Johnsen was about to throw Matt over he pulled out his knife

closed_world.value: Dead Mans Lake and the associated cave system - a forbidden location with supernatural elements
closed_world.quote: It's forbidden for any one to go there

culprit_and_motive.value: Matt killed his grandfather Johnsen in self-defense when Johnsen tried to throw him overboard
culprit_and_motive.quote: Matt realized what Johnsen was going to try to do

investigator.value: No clear investigator present - the narrative reveals the crime through Matt's perspective and supernatural encounters

suspect_architecture.value: Single perpetrator (Matt) with family victims - grandfather Johnsen (murdered), sister Marla (threatened). Limited cast with helper characters (Steve, Alex, Nate) who lack suspect differentiation.
suspect_architecture.quote: Matt had killed him, but of course no one knew that either. Not even his sister Marla.

red_herring.value: The supernatural legend of ol' Johnsen's ghost - deliberate misdirection from the true killer being Matt, his own grandson.
red_herring.quote: The legend says that one black and stormy night an old man went out to sea

clue_fairness.value: Matt's possession of the knife is revealed to reader before any investigation begins, along with his guilt and motive.
clue_fairness.quote: Matt remembered his knife, 'better if I take it along' thought Matt to himself

social_world.value: Family power dynamics - abusive grandfather exploiting vulnerable child, small town community with shared folklore and legends.
social_world.quote: Johnsen grabbed Matt by the arm and yelled 'GO GET DRESSED!!!'

alibi.value: Matt's implicit alibi is that no one would suspect a 10-year-old boy of murder, allowing him to hide in plain sight for years.
alibi.quote: After all who would suspect a 10 year old boy?

media_and_audience.value: Local folklore transforms the crime into supernatural legend, with community creating ghost story to explain Johnsen's disappearance and mysterious reappearance.
media_and_audience.quote: People called him ol' Johnsen... the girl was gone and ol' Johnsen was sitting on a rock whistling

_meta.title: ebook bethany r porter billowing in the distance
